path_after_early_improvement.definition: After a first confirmed improvement, later_new_best means a later passed exact/improved checkpoint with new_score greater than the best confirmed improved score seen so far.

path_after_early_improvement.definition: After a first confirmed improvement, later_new_best means a later passed exact/improved checkpoint with new_score greater than the best confirmed improved score seen so far.
After a first confirmed improvement, later_new_best means a later passed exact/improved checkpoint with new_score greater than the best confirmed improved score seen so far.

metadata.attempt_numbering: Report uses human 1-based attempts; SQLite worker_checkpoints.attempt_index is zero-based.
metadata.later_new_best_definition: After a first confirmed improvement, later_new_best means a later passed exact/improved checkpoint with new_score greater than the best confirmed improved score seen so far.
metadata.previous_epoch_note: The immediate prior epoch is included literally. If it has no confirmed successes, the latest prior result-bearing epoch is also included.

policy.summary: Save/mark first improvement immediately. Do not kill immediately by default; allow up to three follow-up checkpoints after the first improvement, stopping sooner on exact. If no later new best appears within those three follow-ups, stop and move to the next target. If there is no confirmed hit by attempt 5, stop.

recommendations.scope: selected epochs, terminal workers for recommendation; all workers included as live/censored comparison
